opp.next_step: 10% confidence Apr 27 AI appetite. Larger Ai initiative in Sinar mas conglomerate. In parallel, check DC local requirements (mandatory for Indo) Mar 24 Meeting with Head of Strategic Procurement Sinar Mas. Early initiative around spend analysis, AppXtend. opp.ais_why:
• Per the 27 Apr 2026 rep note: 'AI appetite. Larger AI initiative in Sinar Mas conglomerate. In parallel, check DC local requirements (mandatory for Indo).' This is the strongest AI signal in the chunk-4 mid-section ‚Äî rep captures that there is genuine AI appetite linked to a broader conglomerate AI initiative.
• Per the 13 Mar 2026 entry: 'currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities. Demo to be scheduled by mid-April.' On 24 Mar 2026: 'Meeting with Head of Strategic Procurement Sinar Mas. Early initiative around spend analysis, AppXtend. Use cases to be shared by mid of April.' The opp name is 'Sinarmas_Indo_ANA' and the buyer is using Coupa and has expressed direct ANA-capability interest; broader conglomerate AI initiative is the umbrella.
• Confidence is Medium. Sinarmas has explicit ANA interest and a Head of Strategic Procurement meeting completed, plus a conglomerate-wide AI initiative cited as context. Caveat: Products field is null in SFDC (data not yet captured), Indonesia DC location is a mandatory gating requirement, and use cases are still pending sharing. Score 6 reflects buyer-stated AI interest at an early stage without a demo yet completed.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case on record. Next_Step entries reference 'spend analysis' and 'AppXtend' use cases (24 March: 'Early initiative around spend analysis, AppXtend. Use cases to be shared by mid of April'), but the buyer never delivered the promised use-case detail, so scope, savings target, headcount impact, and ROI remain undefined. The 4 May introductory call captured no discussion of quantified pain or metrics. Without buyer-shared use cases and a defined scope, we cannot build or validate a value case. The recommended move is to re-engage Anggita Kristina or Ravinder Mawa to obtain the promised use cases and quantify the business problem (cost, cycle time, compliance risk, or AI-driven savings opportunity) before any demo or commercial step.

sources: Avoma call 4 May 2026 attendees: anggita.kristina@sinarmasmining.com (Strategic BP), eko.lisysantaka@sinarmasmining.com (Dept Head), ravinder.mawa@sinarmasmining.com (CIO/CPO)
Last buyer email 5 May 2026 from Anggita Kristina (Clari/EmailMessage, 57 days ago)
Next_Step__c 27 April: '10% confidence' (owner assessment, implies no champion)
Zero OpportunityContactRole for Champion
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Anggita Kristina (Corporate Procurement Strategic - Business Partner) is the sole active single thread and attended the 4 May introductory call, but she has shown no advocacy behavior (no introductions to power, no internal documents shared, no competitive defense, no agenda-driving). Her last engagement was a buyer email on 5 May (57 days ago), and she has been silent since. No evidence she has access to Ravinder Mawa (CIO/CPO, the likely Economic Buyer) or is pushing the deal internally. Eko Lisysantaka (Dept Head) and Ravinder Mawa attended the intro call but have not engaged since, so champion status is unconfirmed. The owner self-assessed 10% confidence on 27 April, consistent with no champion. The recommended move is to re-engage Anggita and assess her willingness to advocate; if she remains passive, pivot to Ravinder Mawa or Eko Lisysantaka to find a senior sponsor who will drive the evaluation and multi-thread to the broader stakeholder group.

sources: Next_Step__c 13 March 2026: 'currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities'
Products__c: Agentic AI;ANA (our differentiation)
Avoma call 4 May 2026 (no competitive discussion captured)
competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent, confirmed in the Next_Step entry from 13 March: 'currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities.' This positions Zycus as a challenger seeking to displace or augment Coupa with differentiated AI/ANA capabilities (Agentic Negotiation Assistant). No other competitors named in any source. The buyer's interest in Zycus ANA implies a functional gap or dissatisfaction with Coupa's AI-driven sourcing/negotiation features, giving Zycus a wedge. However, replacing an incumbent requires a compelling business case (ROI, quantified pain, AI-driven savings), a strong champion to drive change, and proof that Zycus delivers what Coupa cannot. None of these are in place: no quantified value case, no champion, no demo delivered, and 51 days of buyer silence. Coupa's threat level is medium: they hold the incumbent advantage (switching cost, integration, user adoption), but the buyer's stated interest in our ANA suggests openness to a better AI alternative if we can prove it. The wedge is Zycus Merlin/ANA AI capabilities (agentic negotiation, smarter sourcing automation) vs Coupa's analytics-focused platform. To win, we must deliver the overdue demo, quantify AI-driven ROI (cycle time, savings, negotiation outcomes), and secure a champion who will advocate for the switch.

sources: Next_Step__c 13 March 2026: 'currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities'

identify_pain.narrative: Qualitative pain confirmed but not quantified. The buyer is 'currently using Coupa' (Next_Step 13 March) and expressed 'Interest in our ANA capabilities', implying dissatisfaction or a gap in Coupa's AI-driven sourcing/negotiation features that Zycus ANA addresses. The 24 March entry references 'Early initiative around spend analysis, AppXtend', suggesting pain around spend visibility, analytics depth, or application extensibility that Coupa may not fully solve. The 27 April entry notes 'AI appetite. Larger Ai initiative in Sinar mas conglomerate', framing this as part of a conglomerate-wide AI transformation, which implies pain around manual procurement processes, lack of AI-driven automation, and a strategic imperative to modernize. However, no specific operational pain (cycle time, error rate, compliance risk, supplier management inefficiency) or financial impact (cost, headcount, savings opportunity) has been quantified. The buyer-promised use cases (due mid-April, never delivered) would clarify the pain owner and the quantified impact. Anggita Kristina (Strategic Procurement BP) is the named pain owner based on her role, but she has not engaged in 57 days.

sources: Next_Step__c 27 April 2026: 'check DC local requirements (mandatory for Indo)'
Next_Step__c 20 March 2026: 'broader S2P opportunity at Sinar Mas conglomerate (SG/Indonesia)'
Avoma call 4 May 2026 attendees: techconnect.co.id (potential local partner)
paper_process.narrative: Paper process entirely unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or contracting mechanics captured. A critical InfoSec/compliance requirement surfaced 27 April: Indonesia data-center local-hosting mandate ('check DC local requirements (mandatory for Indo)'), which is a hard gate that must be resolved before any contracting step. If Zycus cannot meet local DC hosting, the deal is disqualified. The broader Sinar Mas conglomerate scope (20 March: SG/Indonesia) implies potential cross-border contracting complexity (which entity signs, multi-jurisdiction legal review), but none of this is mapped. No SI/partner involvement confirmed, though techconnect.co.id attendees (Fernando Natanael, Adrian, Joseph Rianto) on the 4 May call suggest a local implementation partner may be involved. The recommended move is to resolve the Indonesia DC compliance question immediately (go/no-go gate), then map the contracting path (which Sinar Mas entity, legal review SLA, procurement approval chain).

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer confirmed. Ravinder Mawa (CIO & CPO, sinarmasmining.com) attended the 4 May introductory call and holds dual C-level authority (IT and Procurement), making him the likely budget owner for a $100k Agentic AI/ANA deal. However, he has not engaged since that call (57 days ago), no commercial or pricing topics were discussed, and no OpportunityContactRole maps him as EB or Executive Sponsor. Next_Step entries reference a 'broader S2P opportunity at Sinar Mas conglomerate (SG/Indonesia)' (20 March), implying multi-entity scope and potentially higher approval authority (group CFO or conglomerate leadership), but the approval chain is entirely unmapped. The recommended move is to re-engage Ravinder Mawa directly to confirm budget authority for this Indonesia Mining entity scope, and if conglomerate approval is required, map the group-level decision maker.

sources: Next_Step__c 20 March 2026: 'broader S2P opportunity at Sinar Mas conglomerate (SG/Indonesia)'
Avoma call 4 May 2026 attendees: CIO/CPO, Strategic BP, Dept Head (multi-stakeholder, no process discussion)
Next_Step__c 13 March 2026: 'Demo to be scheduled by mid-April' (missed, no follow-up)
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is unmapped. The 4 May introductory call included CIO/CPO Ravinder Mawa, Strategic Procurement BP Anggita Kristina, and Dept Head Eko Lisysantaka, signaling multi-stakeholder evaluation, but no follow-on engagement has occurred to clarify the buying process. The Next_Step entry from 20 March notes this 'can be part of a broader S2P opportunity at Sinar Mas conglomerate (SG/Indonesia)', implying a multi-entity, potentially multi-country scope (Singapore and Indonesia operations), which typically requires conglomerate-level approval and a formal vendor-selection process (RFI, RFP, shortlisting, POC). However, no RFI/RFP timeline, evaluation committee composition, or approval steps have been confirmed. The mid-April demo milestone (logged 13 March) was missed with no reschedule, and the buyer has been silent for 51 days, suggesting either the process has stalled, deprioritized, or moved to other vendors without our visibility. The recommended move is to re-engage and map the full process: who evaluates, who approves, what gates (demo, POC, pricing, InfoSec, legal), and the realistic timeline.

decision_criteria.narrative: Interest confirmed in Zycus ANA (Agentic Negotiation Assistant) capabilities and a broader AI initiative within the Sinar Mas conglomerate (Next_Step 27 April: 'AI appetite. Larger Ai initiative in Sinar mas conglomerate'). The 13 March entry notes they are 'currently using Coupa' and interested in 'our ANA capabilities', suggesting criteria around AI-driven sourcing/negotiation automation and differentiation vs Coupa's analytics. The 24 March entry references 'spend analysis, AppXtend' use cases, indicating a focus on spend visibility and application extensibility. However, formal evaluation criteria (RFI/RFP requirements, scoring rubric, must-have vs nice-to-have features, AI model performance benchmarks, integration requirements) have not been shared. The buyer-promised use cases (due mid-April, never delivered) would clarify decision criteria. A mandatory criterion surfaced 27 April: Indonesia data-center local-hosting compliance ('check DC local requirements (mandatory for Indo)'), unresolved for 8+ weeks and potentially disqualifying if Zycus cannot meet it.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — Coupa is the sole named competitor and holds incumbent status, confirmed 13 March ('currently using Coupa'). Buyer expressed interest in Zycus ANA capabilities to address a functional gap Coupa does not fill, positioning Zycus as a challenger with an AI wedge. Coupa's current threat level is medium: they have switching-cost and integration advantages, but the buyer's stated interest in our ANA signals openness to a better AI alternative. However, 57 days of buyer silence, no delivered demo, no quantified ROI, and no champion mean we have made zero progress against Coupa since the 4 May intro call. To win, we must deliver the overdue demo, quantify AI-driven savings (negotiation outcomes, cycle time, sourcing automation ROI), and secure a champion who will advocate for displacing Coupa.
bullets.text: A competitor is pushing back — Coupa is the sole named competitor and holds incumbent status, confirmed 13 March ('currently using Coupa').

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — Buyer is AI Hungry. Next_Step 27 April confirms 'AI appetite. Larger Ai initiative in Sinar mas conglomerate', and 13 March notes 'Interest in our ANA capabilities'. This positions the deal as part of a strategic conglomerate-wide AI transformation. Zycus Agentic AI and ANA product scope aligns perfectly. However, no AIS score on record (field null), so formal AI excitement tier unconfirmed. The buyer's interest is explicit and current (April 2026), not speculative.

Under-positioned given the buyer's stated AI appetite and the 57-day silence.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 9 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 0 is carried by stage trend +0.33. Offsetting it: momentum 37 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 37 (slipping backward): 57d since a buyer touch (cadence 30d). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.27).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 30 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 2 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 30 and momentum 37, and dragged by risk 9. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal cooling after 51 days of buyer silence following introductory call on 4 May 2026. Owner self-assessed 10% confidence on 27 April. Mid-April demo milestone missed by 10+ weeks, buyer-promised use cases overdue since mid-April, mandatory Indonesia data-center compliance unresolved for 8+ weeks. Single-threaded through Anggita Kristina who last engaged 5 May (email, 57 days ago). No Economic Buyer confirmed, no commercial discussions, and Coupa incumbent in place. December 2026 close requires immediate re-engagement and multi-threading.

flags: Run the overdue mid-April demo with live use-case proof (spend analysis, AppXtend fit, ANA AI-native insights vs Coupa static reporting) to activate competitive differentiation and tie to the conglomerate AI initiative — this is the single highest-leverage move to restart momentum and prove value.

Secure Ravinder Mawa (CIO/CPO) as an active executive sponsor: he attended the 04 May intro but has not re-engaged in 46 days; his dual-hat role (IT + Procurement) makes him the likely pain owner and potential Economic Buyer, but his authority and sponsorship are unconfirmed. Multi-thread to Eko Lisysantaka (Dept Head) in parallel to derisk single-point-of-contact exposure.
best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal cooling after 57 days of buyer silence. Critical to re-engage immediately, resolve go/no-go Indonesia DC compliance, multi-thread beyond single contact, and map Economic Buyer and decision process to salvage December close date.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the sole named competitor and holds incumbent status, confirmed 13 March ('currently using Coupa'). Buyer expressed interest in Zycus ANA capabilities to address a functional gap Coupa does not fill, positioning Zycus as a challenger with an AI wedge. Coupa's current threat level is medium: they have switching-cost and integration advantages, but the buyer's stated interest in our ANA signals openness to a better AI alternative. However, 57 days of buyer silence, no delivered demo, no quantified ROI, and no champion mean we have made zero progress against Coupa since the 4 May intro call. To win, we must deliver the overdue demo, quantify AI-driven savings (negotiation outcomes, cycle time, sourcing automation ROI), and secure a champion who will advocate for displacing Coupa.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities.
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver the overdue ANA demo, quantify AI-driven negotiation ROI (cycle time, savings, sourcing automation outcomes) vs Coupa's analytics-only platform, and secure Ravinder Mawa or Eko Lisysantaka as champion to drive the displacement. Position Zycus Merlin/ANA as the AI-native procurement platform vs Coupa's bolt-on AI.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Buyer expressed strong interest in Zycus ANA (Agentic Negotiation Assistant) capabilities and is part of a larger AI initiative within the Sinar Mas conglomerate (Next_Step 27 April: 'AI appetite. Larger Ai initiative in Sinar mas conglomerate'). Products in scope are Agentic AI and ANA, positioning Zycus as the AI-native procurement platform vs Coupa's analytics-focused incumbent. However, the AI positioning has not been activated: no demo delivered, no Merlin/ANA narrative landed on a call, no AI-driven ROI quantified. Under-positioned given the buyer's stated AI appetite and the 57-day silence.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer expectations center on AI-driven procurement capabilities (ANA, spend analysis, AppXtend) as part of a broader Sinar Mas conglomerate AI initiative. Zycus product scope (Agentic AI, ANA) aligns with stated interest. However, a critical compliance requirement (Indonesia data-center local hosting, mandatory per 27 April Next_Step) is unresolved for 8+ weeks and may be a disqualifying misfit if Zycus cannot deliver. No formal evaluation criteria or use cases shared, so deeper fit (integration, workflow, performance benchmarks) is unconfirmed.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 27 Apr 2026 rep note: 'AI appetite. Larger AI initiative in Sinar Mas conglomerate. In parallel, check DC local requirements (mandatory for Indo).' This is the strongest AI signal in the chunk-4 mid-section ‚Äî rep captures that there is genuine AI appetite linked to a broader conglomerate AI initiative.
• Per the 13 Mar 2026 entry: 'currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities. Demo to be scheduled by mid-April.' On 24 Mar 2026: 'Meeting with Head of Strategic Procurement Sinar Mas. Early initiative around spend analysis, AppXtend. Use cases to be shared by mid of April.' The opp name is 'Sinarmas_Indo_ANA' and the buyer is using Coupa and has expressed direct ANA-capability interest; broader conglomerate AI initiative is the umbrella.
• Confidence is Medium. Sinarmas has explicit ANA interest and a Head of Strategic Procurement meeting completed, plus a conglomerate-wide AI initiative cited as context. Caveat: Products field is null in SFDC (data not yet captured), Indonesia DC location is a mandatory gating requirement, and use cases are still pending sharing. Score 6 reflects buyer-stated AI interest at an early stage without a demo yet completed.
